---
title: "nitrix"
subtitle: "GPU-accelerated pure numerics for neuroimaging in JAX/XLA"
toc: false
---

`nitrix` is the substrate numerics layer of the federation: statistics,
geometry, registration, signal, graph, and morphology, written as pure
JAX/XLA so they run — differentiably, batched, JIT-compiled — on a hardware
accelerator.

::: {.callout-tldr title="Fast path"}
Start with [the statistical modelling suite](explanation/index.qmd) — a textbook
that climbs from generalised linear models up to Gaussian processes for a reader
who knows linear algebra but not formal statistics. Read the
[GLM foundation](explanation/glm.qmd) first; every other model is "GLM plus one
idea".
:::

## Explanation (the textbook layer)

- [The statistical modelling suite](explanation/index.qmd) — the model ladder
  (GLM → LME → GAM → GLMM → GAMM → HSGP) and the shared algorithms behind it.
  - [Generalised linear models: the foundation](explanation/glm.qmd)
  - [Choosing smoothing parameters: the Fellner–Schall method](explanation/fellner-schall.qmd)

More chapters (the rest of the ladder, plus geometry, registration, the semiring
substrate) follow the same pattern: derive the mathematics, justify the design,
point at the code.
